title: celtis pallida subsp. sericea
intro: Celtis pallida subsp. sericea, also known as Silver-leaved Netleaf Hackberry, is a subspecies of the Celtis pallida plant native to Western Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its bark and leaves. The Silver-leaved Netleaf Hackberry has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, with its leaves and bark providing relief and promoting overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
